personal.name: S Prajwall Narayana
personal.title: AI Engineer, Full-Stack & DevOps Engineer
personal.tagline: Building autonomous app-generation systems and self-healing deployment infrastructure

about.title: Current Work
about.description: AI Engineer at Colligence Research architecting autonomous compilers with Claude Code integration for self-healing deployment pipelines. Building DevOps infrastructure using Docker, Ansible, OpenTofu, and Linode with microfrontend architecture and Citadel auth.

about.title: Research
about.description: 2 IEEE Publications on robotics and ML. Built YOLOv3 rover with 4-DOF robotic arm (published IEEE Xplore Nov 2024) and ResNet50 hyperparameter optimization for plant disease classification.

about.title: Impact
about.description: Platforms serving 1,000+ students (KnotesCentral). 93% accuracy deepfake detection on FaceForensics++ and DFDC benchmarks. 30-language healthcare system (Arogya-Sathi) with symptom checker and X-ray analyzer.

experience.title: AI Engineer, Prompt Engineering Researcher & DevOps/Infra
experience.description: Architecting autonomous compilers with Claude Code integration for self-healing deployment pipelines. Building DevOps infrastructure using Docker, Ansible, OpenTofu, and Linode with microfrontend architecture and Citadel auth.

experience.title: Student Intern - AI Research
experience.description: Developed deepfake detection model using CNN architectures achieving 92.3% accuracy, 90.7% precision, and 91.5% recall on FaceForensics++ and DFDC benchmarks.

experience.title: Associate Consultant Intern
experience.description: Gained expertise in Enterprise Security Architecture and Cybersecurity through work on the IFTAS Cloud project. Applied the SABSA Framework for risk-driven design, cloud security, IAM, and regulatory compliance.

education.title: Bachelor of Engineering - Computer Science
education.description: Computer Science graduate from RVCE. Completed coursework in Data Structures, Algorithms, Database Management, Software Engineering, and Computer Networks.
